stories.problemStatement: The current backlog refinement discussion describes scattered refinement notes that need to be converted into a sprint-ready backlog artifact. This matters because the team needs a consistent, reviewable story before work can be committed. The purpose of this work is to turn the discussion into a clear refinement package. The discussion already identifies 2 ordered acceptance criteria.
stories.storySummary: As a refinement facilitator, I want to turn messy refinement notes into a structured story package so that I can help the team commit work with clear scope and testable acceptance criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: Given messy refinement notes, when the facilitator submits them, then the response returns the canonical story package with problemStatement, storySummary, acceptanceCriteria, dependencies, references, estimation, definitionOfReadyValidation, gaps, and normalizationNotes in that order.
Should flag a misalignment when the notes describe one workstream but the story summary focuses on another.
Should add DoR gaps for missing parent epic link, labels, sprint, or estimate information.
definitionOfReadyValidation: [x] Problem statement present and aligned with story
[x] User story follows "As a / I want / So that" format
[x] Acceptance criteria are present and testable
[x] Estimation <= 5 story points (if larger, recommend splitting)
gaps: ⚠️ DoR GAP: problem statement was not explicitly provided in the refinement notes
normalizationNotes: Collapsed whitespace and removed empty lines from the refinement notes.
Generated a canonical story summary because no candidate summary was provided.
Surface any unresolved gaps before sprint commitment.
stories.label: refinement
